chief_complaint: A 3-year-old female presented with chronic anterior chest wall pain.
history: The patient had a 3-year history of chronic anterior chest wall pain attributed to a bifid right fourth rib. Imaging confirmed the anomaly without associated soft tissue masses. She had transient symptom relief with lidocaine patches and intercostal nerve blocks.
exam: Vital signs were within normal limits. Physical examination revealed tenderness over the right anterior chest wall.
labs: Imaging studies: chest X-ray and CT scan confirmed the bifid right fourth rib. ECG and blood tests were normal.
